forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8980'
+project: semantic-search
+title: Code-aware tokenizer module for search fusion
+---
+## What
+Add a code-aware tokenizer that the BM25 and trigram signals of the fused `search code` will share. New module: `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/tokenize.rs` (create the `search_fusion` submodule dir; register `pub mod search_fusion;` inside `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_code.rs` OR add `pub mod search_fusion;` to `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/mod.rs` â€” pick the latter so the primitives are reusable and unit-testable independently of the op).
+
+Tokenization rules (agreed design â€” do NOT add Porter stemming; trigram carries fuzziness):
+- Lowercase everything.
+- Split on whitespace and punctuation (anything not `[A-Za-z0-9]`).
+- Additionally split identifiers on camelCase boundaries (`getUser` -> `get`, `user`) and snake_case boundaries (`get_user` -> `get`, `user`).
+- Digit runs split off as their own tokens at letter/digit transitions is optional â€” keep simple: treat `[A-Za-z0-9]+` runs then sub-split on camelCase. Document the exact rule chosen in the function docstring.
+
+Public API (pure, no DB):
+- `pub fn tokenize(text: &str) -> Vec<String>` â€” full pipeline above.
+- `pub fn char_trigrams(s: &str) -> Vec<[char; 3]>` (or `Vec<String>` of length-3 windows) over a lowercased string â€” used by the trigram-Dice signal. Document handling of strings shorter than 3 chars (return empty).
+
+This is foundational: BM25 (corpus df/avgdl + per-chunk tf) and trigram-Dice both consume `tokenize`. No DB access, no embedding access â€” pure string functions.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `tokenize("getUserById")` yields `["get", "user", "by", "id"]` (camelCase split, lowercased).
+- [ ] `tokenize("get_user_by_id")` yields `["get", "user", "by", "id"]` (snake_case split).
+- [ ] `tokenize("fn parse_config() -> Result")` splits punctuation and keywords into lowercase word tokens with no empty strings.
+- [ ] `char_trigrams("get_user")` returns the sliding length-3 windows of the lowercased string; strings shorter than 3 chars return an empty Vec.
+- [ ] Module is reachable from `search_code.rs` (it `use`s `super::search_fusion::tokenize`).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/tokenize.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: camelCase split, snake_case split, mixed `getHTTPResponse`-style runs, punctuation stripping, empty-string and sub-3-char trigram edge cases.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-code-context tokenize` passes (all new tests green).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-title: Code-aware tokenizer module for search fusion
+title: 'Create swissarmyhammer-search crate: types + code-aware tokenizer'
 ---
 ## What
-Add a code-aware tokenizer that the BM25 and trigram signals of the fused `search code` will share. New module: `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/tokenize.rs` (create the `search_fusion` submodule dir; register `pub mod search_fusion;` inside `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_code.rs` OR add `pub mod search_fusion;` to `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/mod.rs` â€” pick the latter so the primitives are reusable and unit-testable independently of the op).
+Scaffold a NEW leaf crate `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/` â€” a generic, in-memory document ranker with NO workspace dependencies (true leaf). It is consumed by BOTH `swissarmyhammer-code-context` (chunk search) and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` (task search), so it must NOT depend on `model-embedding`, `swissarmyhammer-embedding`, or any sibling crate.
+
+Files to create:
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/Cargo.toml` â€” package `swissarmyhammer-search`, `version.workspace = true`, edition/license/repository `.workspace = true`. Deps: `serde = { workspace = true }` only (for `Serialize` on `Hit`/`Signals`). No tokio, no model crates.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/lib.rs` â€” module decls + the public types below.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/tokenize.rs` â€” the tokenizer + `char_trigrams`.
+
+Workspace wiring (edit `Cargo.toml` at repo root):
+- Add `"crates/swissarmyhammer-search"` to `[workspace] members`.
+- Add `swissarmyhammer-search = { path = "crates/swissarmyhammer-search" }` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (alphabetical, near the other `swissarmyhammer-*` entries around the `swissarmyhammer-sem` / `swissarmyhammer-skills` lines).

-Tokenization rules (agreed design â€” do NOT add Porter stemming; trigram carries fuzziness):
-- Lowercase everything.
-- Split on whitespace and punctuation (anything not `[A-Za-z0-9]`).
-- Additionally split identifiers on camelCase boundaries (`getUser` -> `get`, `user`) and snake_case boundaries (`get_user` -> `get`, `user`).
-- Digit runs split off as their own tokens at letter/digit transitions is optional â€” keep simple: treat `[A-Za-z0-9]+` runs then sub-split on camelCase. Document the exact rule chosen in the function docstring.
+Public types (in `lib.rs`; this card defines them, the scoring card implements `search()`):
+- `pub struct Doc { pub id: String, pub fields: Vec<Field>, pub embedding: Option<Vec<f32>> }`
+- `pub struct Field { pub weight: f32, pub text: String }`
+- `pub struct Query { pub text: String, pub embedding: Option<Vec<f32>>, pub weights: SignalWeights, pub top_k: usize, pub min_score: Option<f32> }`
+- `pub struct SignalWeights { pub w_bm25: f32, pub w_trigram: f32, pub w_cosine: f32 }` with a `Default` of all `1.0`.
+- `pub struct Hit { pub id: String, pub score: f32, pub signals: Signals }` â€” derive `Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize`.
+- `pub struct Signals { pub bm25: f32, pub trigram: f32, pub cosine: f32 }` â€” derive `Debug, Clone, Copy, serde::Serialize`.

-Public API (pure, no DB):
-- `pub fn tokenize(text: &str) -> Vec<String>` â€” full pipeline above.
-- `pub fn char_trigrams(s: &str) -> Vec<[char; 3]>` (or `Vec<String>` of length-3 windows) over a lowercased string â€” used by the trigram-Dice signal. Document handling of strings shorter than 3 chars (return empty).
+Tokenizer (`tokenize.rs`, pure functions, no DB/embedding access â€” do NOT add Porter stemming; trigram carries fuzziness):
+- `pub fn tokenize(text: &str) -> Vec<String>`:
+  - Lowercase everything.
+  - Split on whitespace and punctuation (anything not `[A-Za-z0-9]`).
+  - Sub-split each `[A-Za-z0-9]+` run on camelCase boundaries (`getUser` -> `get`, `user`) and snake_case boundaries (already split by punctuation since `_` is punctuation). Document the exact rule chosen in the docstring (e.g. split at lowerâ†’upper transitions, and at letterâ†’digit if chosen â€” keep it documented and consistent).
+  - No empty strings in the output.
+- `pub fn char_trigrams(s: &str) -> Vec<[char; 3]>` over a lowercased string â€” sliding length-3 windows. Strings shorter than 3 chars return an empty Vec. Document the behavior.

-This is foundational: BM25 (corpus df/avgdl + per-chunk tf) and trigram-Dice both consume `tokenize`. No DB access, no embedding access â€” pure string functions.
+This is foundational and reusable: BM25 (corpus df/avgdl + per-doc tf) and trigram-Dice both consume `tokenize`/`char_trigrams` in the scoring card.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/` exists, is a workspace member, and `cargo build -p swissarmyhammer-search` compiles with only `serde` as a dependency.
 - [ ] `tokenize("getUserById")` yields `["get", "user", "by", "id"]` (camelCase split, lowercased).
-- [ ] `tokenize("get_user_by_id")` yields `["get", "user", "by", "id"]` (snake_case split).
-- [ ] `tokenize("fn parse_config() -> Result")` splits punctuation and keywords into lowercase word tokens with no empty strings.
+- [ ] `tokenize("get_user_by_id")` yields `["get", "user", "by", "id"]` (snake_case split via punctuation).
+- [ ] `tokenize("fn parse_config() -> Result")` splits punctuation/keywords into lowercase word tokens with no empty strings.
 - [ ] `char_trigrams("get_user")` returns the sliding length-3 windows of the lowercased string; strings shorter than 3 chars return an empty Vec.
-- [ ] Module is reachable from `search_code.rs` (it `use`s `super::search_fusion::tokenize`).
+- [ ] All public types (`Doc`/`Field`/`Query`/`SignalWeights`/`Hit`/`Signals`) compile and `Hit`/`Signals` are `Serialize`; `SignalWeights::default()` is all `1.0`.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/tokenize.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: camelCase split, snake_case split, mixed `getHTTPResponse`-style runs, punctuation stripping, empty-string and sub-3-char trigram edge cases.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-code-context tokenize` passes (all new tests green).
+- [ ] Unit tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/tokenize.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: camelCase split, snake_case split, mixed `getHTTPResponse`-style runs, punctuation stripping, empty-string and sub-3-char trigram edge cases.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-search tokenize` passes (all new tests green).

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,16 +7,21 @@
 title: 'Create swissarmyhammer-search crate: types + code-aware tokenizer'
 ---
 ## What
-Scaffold a NEW leaf crate `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/` â€” a generic, in-memory document ranker with NO workspace dependencies (true leaf). It is consumed by BOTH `swissarmyhammer-code-context` (chunk search) and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` (task search), so it must NOT depend on `model-embedding`, `swissarmyhammer-embedding`, or any sibling crate.
+Scaffold a NEW leaf crate `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/` â€” a generic, in-memory document ranker with NO workspace-sibling dependencies (true leaf). It is consumed by BOTH `swissarmyhammer-code-context` (chunk search) and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` (task search), so it must NOT depend on `model-embedding`, `swissarmyhammer-embedding`, or any sibling crate.
+
+DO NOT hand-roll the word/identifier splitter. Use existing, dependency-light crates already resolved in the workspace `Cargo.lock`:
+- `convert_case` (0.10) â€” `convert_case::split(identifier, boundaries) -> Vec<&str>` with the `Boundary` enum. Handles camelCase/PascalCase, acronyms (`HTTPRequest` -> `http`,`request`), and digit boundaries. We choose the `Boundary` set, so digit-splitting is a config choice, not code.
+- `unicode-segmentation` (1.13) â€” `UnicodeSegmentation::unicode_words()` (UAX #29) for the punctuation/whitespace/Unicode word layer (zero transitive deps).

 Files to create:
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/Cargo.toml` â€” package `swissarmyhammer-search`, `version.workspace = true`, edition/license/repository `.workspace = true`. Deps: `serde = { workspace = true }` only (for `Serialize` on `Hit`/`Signals`). No tokio, no model crates.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/Cargo.toml` â€” package `swissarmyhammer-search`, `version.workspace = true`, edition/license/repository `.workspace = true`. Deps: `serde = { workspace = true }` (for `Serialize` on `Hit`/`Signals`), `convert_case = { workspace = true }`, `unicode-segmentation = { workspace = true }`. No tokio, no model crates, no sibling crates.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/lib.rs` â€” module decls + the public types below.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/tokenize.rs` â€” the tokenizer + `char_trigrams`.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/tokenize.rs` â€” the tokenizer (wrapping the two crates) + `char_trigrams`.

 Workspace wiring (edit `Cargo.toml` at repo root):
 - Add `"crates/swissarmyhammer-search"` to `[workspace] members`.
-- Add `swissarmyhammer-search = { path = "crates/swissarmyhammer-search" }` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (alphabetical, near the other `swissarmyhammer-*` entries around the `swissarmyhammer-sem` / `swissarmyhammer-skills` lines).
+- Add `swissarmyhammer-search = { path = "crates/swissarmyhammer-search" }` to `[workspace.dependencies]` (alphabetical, near the other `swissarmyhammer-*` entries).
+- Ensure `convert_case` and `unicode-segmentation` are present in `[workspace.dependencies]` (add them pinned to the already-resolved versions `convert_case = "0.10"`, `unicode-segmentation = "1"` if not already listed â€” they exist transitively in `Cargo.lock` today).

 Public types (in `lib.rs`; this card defines them, the scoring card implements `search()`):
 - `pub struct Doc { pub id: String, pub fields: Vec<Field>, pub embedding: Option<Vec<f32>> }`
@@ -26,26 +31,28 @@
 - `pub struct Hit { pub id: String, pub score: f32, pub signals: Signals }` â€” derive `Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize`.
 - `pub struct Signals { pub bm25: f32, pub trigram: f32, pub cosine: f32 }` â€” derive `Debug, Clone, Copy, serde::Serialize`.

-Tokenizer (`tokenize.rs`, pure functions, no DB/embedding access â€” do NOT add Porter stemming; trigram carries fuzziness):
+Tokenizer (`tokenize.rs`, pure functions, no DB/embedding access â€” NO Porter stemming; trigram carries fuzziness):
 - `pub fn tokenize(text: &str) -> Vec<String>`:
-  - Lowercase everything.
-  - Split on whitespace and punctuation (anything not `[A-Za-z0-9]`).
-  - Sub-split each `[A-Za-z0-9]+` run on camelCase boundaries (`getUser` -> `get`, `user`) and snake_case boundaries (already split by punctuation since `_` is punctuation). Document the exact rule chosen in the docstring (e.g. split at lowerâ†’upper transitions, and at letterâ†’digit if chosen â€” keep it documented and consistent).
-  - No empty strings in the output.
-- `pub fn char_trigrams(s: &str) -> Vec<[char; 3]>` over a lowercased string â€” sliding length-3 windows. Strings shorter than 3 chars return an empty Vec. Document the behavior.
+  - Pass `text` through `unicode_words()` to get word runs (strips punctuation/whitespace, Unicode-correct).
+  - For each word, apply `convert_case::split(word, &BOUNDARIES)` to break camelCase/PascalCase/acronym/underscore boundaries.
+  - Define a module-level `BOUNDARIES` slice (the agreed decision): include case + acronym + delimiter boundaries (`LowerUpper`, `UpperLower`/`Acronym`, `Underscore`, `Hyphen`, `Space`); EXCLUDE the digit boundaries so `sha256`/`utf8`/`base64` stay whole. Document the exact slice in the docstring.
+  - Lowercase each segment; preserve duplicates (BM25 needs term frequency â€” do NOT dedup); no empty strings.
+- `pub fn char_trigrams(s: &str) -> Vec<[char; 3]>` over a lowercased string â€” sliding length-3 windows of `chars()` (Unicode-safe). Strings shorter than 3 chars return an empty Vec. (This is n-gram windowing, not a splitter â€” keep inline.)

 This is foundational and reusable: BM25 (corpus df/avgdl + per-doc tf) and trigram-Dice both consume `tokenize`/`char_trigrams` in the scoring card.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/` exists, is a workspace member, and `cargo build -p swissarmyhammer-search` compiles with only `serde` as a dependency.

+Tokenizer (`tokenize.rs`, pure functions, no DB/embedding access â€” do NOT add Porter stemming; trigram carries fuzziness):
 - `pub fn tokenize(text: &str) -> Vec<String>`:
-  - Pass `text` through `unicode_words()` to get word runs (strips punctuation/whitespace, Unicode-correct).
-  - For each word, apply `convert_case::split(word, &BOUNDARIES)` to break camelCase/PascalCase/acronym/underscore boundaries.
-  - Define a module-level `BOUNDARIES` slice (the agreed decision): include case + acronym + delimiter boundaries (`LowerUpper`, `UpperLower`/`Acronym`, `Underscore`, `Hyphen`, `Space`); EXCLUDE the digit boundaries so `sha256`/`utf8`/`base64` stay whole. Document the exact slice in the docstring.

-- `pub fn char_trigrams(s: &str) -> Vec<[char; 3]>` over a lowercased string â€” sliding length-3 windows of `chars()` (Unicode-safe). Strings shorter than 3 chars return an empty Vec. (This is n-gram windowing, not a splitter â€” keep inline.)
+  - Lowercase everything.
+  - Split on whitespace and punctuation (anything not `[A-Za-z0-9]`).
+  - Sub-split each `[A-Za-z0-9]+` run on camelCase boundaries (`getUser` -> `get`, `user`) and snake_case boundaries (already split by punctuation since `_` is punctuation). +  - No empty strings in the output.
